title: The Ninth-Grade Shock: Why Mindset Matters Most
subtitle: 九年级冲击波：思维模式决定成败

paragraphs.en: This month, more than 4 million students across the nation will begin high school. Many will do well. But many will not.
paragraphs.zh: 本月，全美将有超过四百万学生步入高中校门。其中许多人会表现优异，但也有许多人不会。

paragraphs.en: Consider that nearly two-thirds of students will experience the "ninth-grade shock," which refers to a dramatic drop in a student's academic performance. Some students cope with this shock by avoiding challenges. For instance, they may drop difficult coursework.
paragraphs.zh: 要知道，近三分之二的学生将经历“九年级冲击”，即学业成绩的急剧下滑。一些学生通过逃避挑战来应对这一冲击，例如放弃难度较高的课程。

paragraphs.en: Others may experience a hopelessness that results in failing their core classes, such as English, science and math. This should matter a great deal to parents, teachers and policymakers. Ultimately it should matter to the students themselves and society at large, because students' experience of transitioning to the ninth grade can have long-term consequences not only for the students themselves but for their home communities.
paragraphs.zh: 另一些学生则可能陷入绝望，导致英语、科学和数学等核心课程不及格。这理应引起家长、教师和政策制定者的高度重视。归根结底，学生自身乃至整个社会都应关注此事，因为向九年级过渡的经历不仅关乎学生个人，还可能对所在社区产生长远影响。

paragraphs.en: We make these observations as research psychologists who have studied how schools and families can help young people thrive. In the new global economy, students who fail to finish the ninth grade with passing grades in college preparatory coursework are very unlikely to graduate on time and go on to get jobs.
paragraphs.zh: 我们是研究学校和家庭如何帮助青少年茁壮成长的心理学家，这些观察均源于我们的研究。在新的全球经济环境下，若九年级学生未能以合格成绩完成大学预科课程，他们极有可能无法按时毕业并顺利就业。

paragraphs.en: One study has calculated that the lifetime benefit to the local economy for a single additional student who completes high school is half a million dollars or more. This is based on higher earnings and avoided costs in health care, crime, welfare dependence and other things.
paragraphs.zh: 一项研究估算，每多一名完成高中学业的学生，其对当地经济的终身贡献价值可达五十万美元甚至更高。这一数字基于更高的收入以及在医疗、犯罪、福利依赖等方面节省的开支。

paragraphs.en: The consequences of doing poorly in the ninth grade can impact more than students' ability to find a good job. It can also impact the extent to which they enjoy life. Students lose many of the friends they turned to for support when they move from the eighth to the ninth grade.
paragraphs.zh: 九年级表现不佳的后果远不止影响学生找份好工作的能力，还会影响他们享受生活的程度。从八年级升入九年级时，学生会失去许多曾赖以支撑的朋友。

paragraphs.en: One study of ninth-grade students found that 50 percent of friendships among ninth graders changed from one month to the next, signaling striking instability in friendships. In addition, studies find the first year of high school typically shows one of the greatest increases in depression of any year over the lifespan.
paragraphs.zh: 一项针对九年级学生的研究发现，九年级生之间50%的友谊在短短一个月内就会发生变化，表明其友谊极不稳定。此外，研究还发现高中第一年通常是人生各阶段中抑郁症状增幅最大的年份之一。

paragraphs.en: Researchers think that one explanation is that ties to friends are broken while academic demands are rising. Furthermore, most adult cases of clinical depression first emerge in adolescence. The World Health Organization reports that depression has the greatest burden of disease worldwide, in terms of the total cost of treatment and the loss of productivity.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人员认为，原因之一在于学业压力攀升的同时，与朋友的纽带却遭撕裂。而且，多数成人临床抑郁症病例最初都出现于青春期。世界卫生组织报告称，就治疗总费用与生产力损失而言，抑郁症已成为全球疾病负担最重的疾病。

paragraphs.en: Given all that's riding on having a successful ninth grade experience, it pays to explore what can be done to meet the academic, social and emotional challenges of the transition to high school. So far, our studies have yielded one main insight: Students' beliefs about change—their beliefs about whether people are stuck one way forever, or whether people can change their personalities and abilities—are related to their ability to cope, succeed academically and maintain good mental health.
paragraphs.zh: 鉴于成功的九年级经历至关重要，探索如何应对高中过渡期在学业、社交与情感方面的种种挑战便颇具价值。迄今为止，我们的研究得出了一条核心洞见：学生关于改变的信念——即他们是否认为人永远一成不变，还是可以改变自身性格与能力——与其应对能力、学业成就及心理健康密切相关。

paragraphs.en: Past research has called these beliefs "mindsets," with a "fixed mindset" referring to the belief that people cannot change and a "growth mindset" referring to the belief that people can change. In one recent study, we examined 360 adolescents' beliefs about the nature of "smartness"—that is, their fixed mindsets about intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 过往研究将这些信念称为“思维模式”，其中“固定型思维模式”指认为人无法改变的信念，而“成长型思维模式”则指认为人可以改变的信念。在最近一项研究中，我们考察了360名青少年对“聪明”本质的看法，亦即他们关于智力的固定型思维模式。

paragraphs.en: We then assessed biological stress responses for students whose grades were dropping by examining their stress hormones. Students who believed that intelligence is fixed—that you are stuck being "not smart" if you struggle in school—showed higher levels of stress hormones when their grades were declining at the beginning of the ninth grade.
paragraphs.zh: 接着，我们通过检测压力激素来评估成绩下滑学生的生理应激反应。那些认为智力固定不变的学生——即认为在校遇到困难就意味着自己“不聪明”——在九年级初期成绩下降时，体内压力激素水平更高。

paragraphs.en: If students believed that intelligence could improve—that is to say, when they held more of a growth mindset of intelligence—they showed lower levels of stress hormones when their grades were declining. This was an exciting result because it showed that the body's stress responses are not determined solely by one's grades.
paragraphs.zh: 如果学生相信智力可以提高——也就是说，当他们更多持有成长型智力思维模式时——成绩下降时的压力激素水平则更低。这一结果令人振奋，因为它表明身体的应激反应并非单由成绩决定。

paragraphs.en: Instead, declining grades only predicted worse stress hormones among students who believed that worsening grades were a permanent and hopeless state of affairs. We also investigated the social side of the high school transition.
paragraphs.zh: 相反，只有当学生认为成绩恶化是一种永久且无望的状态时，成绩下滑才预示着更糟的压力激素反应。我们还对高中过渡期的社交层面进行了研究。

paragraphs.en: In this study, instead of teaching students that their smartness can change, we taught them that their social standing—that is, whether they are bullied or excluded or left out—can change over time. We then looked at high school students' stress responses to daily social difficulties. That is, we taught them a growth mindset about their social lives.
paragraphs.zh: 在这项研究中，我们没有教导学生聪明程度可以改变，而是告诉他们社交地位——即他们是否受欺负、被排斥或遭冷落——是可以随时间变化的。然后我们观察了高中生面对日常社交困难时的应激反应。换言之，我们教给了他们一种关于社交生活的成长型思维模式。

paragraphs.en: In this study, students came into the laboratory and were asked to give a public speech in front of upper-year students. The topic of the speech was what makes one popular in high school. Following this, students had to complete a difficult mental math task in front of the same upper-year students.
paragraphs.zh: 研究中，学生们来到实验室，被要求在高年级学生面前发表一场公开演讲，主题是何为高中受欢迎之道。之后，他们还须在同一批高年级学生面前完成一项困难的心算任务。

paragraphs.en: Experiment results showed that students who were not taught that people can change showed poor stress responses. When these students gave the speech, their blood vessels contracted and their hearts pumped less blood through the body—both responses that the body shows when it is preparing for damage or defeat after a physical threat.
paragraphs.zh: 实验结果显示，未被告知人可以改变的学生表现出较差的应激反应。当这些学生发表演讲时，他们的血管收缩，心脏泵出的血液减少——这两种反应均是身体在面临生理威胁、预备承受伤害或失败时的表现。

paragraphs.en: Then they gave worse speeches and made more mistakes in math. But when students were taught that people can change, they had better responses to stress, in part because they felt like they had the resources to deal with the demanding situation.
paragraphs.zh: 随后他们的演讲表现更糟，数学计算错误也更多。然而，当学生被告知人是可以改变的之后，他们对压力的反应就好得多，部分原因在于他们感觉自己拥有应对这一高要求情境的资本。

paragraphs.en: Students who got the growth mindset intervention showed less-contracted blood vessels and their hearts pumped more blood—both of which contributed to more oxygen getting to the brain, and, ultimately, better performance on the speech and mental math tasks. These findings lead to several possibilities that we are investigating further.
paragraphs.zh: 接受了成长型思维干预的学生血管收缩程度更轻，心脏泵出更多血液——两者均有助于更多氧气输往大脑，最终在演讲与心算任务中取得更佳表现。这些发现引出了若干我们正在进一步探究的可能性。

paragraphs.en: First, we are working to replicate these findings in more diverse school communities. We want to know in which types of schools and for which kinds of students these growth mindset ideas help young people adapt to the challenges of high school.
paragraphs.zh: 首先，我们正致力于在更为多元的学校环境中复现这些发现。我们想了解，在何种类型的学校中、对哪类学生而言，成长型思维理念有助于年轻人适应高中挑战。

paragraphs.en: We also hope to learn how teachers, parents or school counselors can help students keep their ongoing academic or social difficulties in perspective. We wonder what would happen if schools helped to make beliefs about the potential for change and improvement a larger feature of the overall school culture, especially for students starting the ninth grade.
paragraphs.zh: 我们还希望了解，教师、家长或学校辅导员如何能帮助学生正确看待持续存在的学业或社交困难。我们好奇，若学校能将关于改变与进步潜力的信念融入整体校园文化，尤其是面向刚升入九年级的学生，会产生怎样的效果。
